# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image  
FROM python:2.7-slim       # Change to your preferred base docker file, e.g., node or alpine based on requirement 
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED True   
WORKDIR /app        # Define working directory of the container - you can change this as per need  
                  
# Install production dependencies (the requirements for our multi-service application)    
COPY requirements.txt ./     
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt 

# Copy project files from your local system to docker image    # For multiple service, use the same Dockerfile and adjust copy statements  
ADD . /app      
WORKDIR /app        # Set working directory inside container for each respective services    
RUN pip install --no-cache -e ".[webserver]"# Install web server dependencies 
# Run your application or write command to start service here    e.g., python manage.py runserver:80 &   pyhton myapp.py serve # Start API Server and Database Service, you can use this for other services too as well based on requirement      
EXPOSE 80          # Expose port where application will listen to requests from outside the container    
# For DB service            EXPOSE 5432    if your database is listening in a specific host/port  
                 # Add more Ports here for other services also. If you are using Docker Compose, it's common practice not only expose ports but to make sure that they can be reached from outside the container as well      
# For Redis service            EXPOSE 6379    if your redis is listening in a specific host/port  
                 # Add more Ports here for other services also. If you are using Docker Compose, it's common practice not only expose ports but to make sure that they can be reached from outside the container as well      
# Run any commands or scripts required at start of your service in separate lines below this line    e.g., RUN echo "Starting web server"   # Make use appropriate instructions for starting services here based on requirement     
                	# End all necessary exposes and run command to create a container  - Uncomment as per requiremnts       Run $ docker-compose up if you are using Docker Compose else un comment the below line.     RUN ["docker-entrypoint.sh"]   # To start services inside an already running or detached containers, use this point and provide appropriate command
